hard.next_step: Timothy Duprey (Category Manager) wants to understand how AI tools can help his procurement role; cites tail-spend leakage, off-contract buying despite Ariba P2P, poor end-to-end visibility, Ariba integration stalled by S/4HANA migration, Jagger used for RFX, Sirion for CLM; estimates a formal project may form around 2026.
hard.ais_why: Chemours category manager Timothy openly asked how AI tools can help his role and framed AI as a way to 'catch up and be more efficient', tied to Ariba/S4HANA migration frustration; a formal project is only expected to form around 2026 (last activity 24 Jun 2026).
hard.competitor: SAP Ariba (incumbent); Jagger/JAGGAER (incumbent RFX); Sirion (incumbent CLM); do-nothing / status quo
hard.primary_competitor: Do-nothing / status quo (incumbent Ariba + Jagger + Sirion)

north_star_verdict.headline: One 16 Sep 2025 discovery call with a self-described non-decision-maker, then silence: three 2026 rep emails (24 Mar, 20 May, 24 Jun) drew no reply, no project was chartered, and the close date was quietly pushed to 8 Jan 2027. Buyer engagement is cold; re-establishing two-way contact is the only real move.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 16 Sep 2025, in Qualified ~9.5 months. Close date pushed from 2026-09-16 to 2027-01-08 on 30 Jun 2026 by the owner (a placeholder, no buyer commitment), now 186 days out. The last GENUINE two-way buyer exchange was the 16 Sep 2025 call, ~9.5 months ago; the three 2026 activities are all Zycus-sent Clari emails with no buyer reply, so buyer engagement (not rep outreach) has been zero for well over 60 days. Cold 60+ days forces Off Track regardless of stage. No EB/DM/project leader mapped, no RFP cycle, no commercials, no second meeting.
evidence: Stage: Qualified (early discovery, no formal project chartered)
Last genuine two-way buyer exchange: 16 Sep 2025 discovery call (~9.5 months ago)
2026 activity is rep-side only: Clari emails 24 Mar, 20 May, 24 Jun 2026, all unanswered by the buyer
Timothy Duprey said on 16 Sep 2025 he is 'not driving anything, only a stakeholder' and 'not part of the CET governing project priorities'
Close date pushed 2026-09-16 to 2027-01-08 on 30 Jun 2026 by Edward Dlugosz (a slip, not buyer-driven)
No economic buyer, decision maker, or project leader mapped; no RFP/RFQ, no commercials, no contract draft
Formal project only ever estimated as 'maybe 2026' by a non-decision-making contact

metrics.narrative: Timothy Duprey articulated clear operational pain on the 16 Sep 2025 call but never validated a financial value case. He stated Chemours' tail spend is 'probably 80 to a 100,000,000', and the rep positioned Anna's 4.5-9% tail-spend savings as roughly $4-8M for Chemours, but Timothy only replied 'Oh, yeah' and never confirmed or owned that number. No cost of the current pain (maverick-spend leakage rate, duplicate-system overhead, cycle-time cost) has been quantified by the buyer. The move is to co-build a buyer-validated tail-spend savings model Timothy can carry to the CET.
sources: Avoma discovery call 16 Sep 2025, Timothy Duprey: 'it's probably 80 to a 100,000,000'
Avoma 16 Sep 2025, Zycus rep positioned 4.5-9% ($4-8M) Anna tail-spend savings; not buyer-validated

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified or engaged. On 16 Sep 2025 Timothy Duprey explicitly placed authority elsewhere: 'I'm not the project leader, part of the CET governing the project priorities' and 'I'm not driving anything, but I'm a stakeholder.' The rep's own prep targeted the unnamed CPO's pain from Chemours' annual report, but no CPO, CFO, or CET project owner has ever been contacted. Budget is hostage to the S/4HANA migration, which previously halted procurement progress. The move is to use Timothy to name the CET project owner and secure a warm introduction.
sources: Avoma discovery call 16 Sep 2025, Timothy Duprey: 'I'm not the project leader, part of the CET governing the project priorities'
No OpportunityContactRole EB/Exec-Sponsor on the opp

decision_criteria.narrative: No documented BRD or RFP criteria exist; criteria are inferred from Timothy's reactions on 16 Sep 2025. Signals that will matter: platform consolidation (he described the pain of Ariba + Jagger + Sirion running as 'separate databases going in and out with each of its own challenges'), AI/automation and adoption (positive reaction to Merlin Intake on MS Teams and Anna for tail spend), CLM usability ('you don't get an effective dashboard' from Sirion), and independence from the S/4HANA migration. All are inferred, not confirmed against a formal evaluation.
sources: Avoma discovery call 16 Sep 2025, Timothy Duprey on fragmentation, Sirion dashboards, AI interest

decision_process.narrative: Read from the single call: Chemours charters procurement projects through a cross-functional 'CET' governance team that sets priorities; Timothy is stakeholder/end-user input, not a driver. A prior evaluation is telling: the rep noted 'you guys doing an evaluation a couple years back... you just kept it', so the buyer's historical process ended in keeping Ariba. No RFI/RFP has started and a formal project was only estimated for 'maybe 2026'. Making the CET process and its owner visible is the prerequisite to any real path.
sources: Avoma discovery call 16 Sep 2025, Timothy Duprey: 'part of the CET governing the project priorities'; rep: 'an evaluation a couple years back... you just kept it'

paper_process.narrative: Nothing has started on paper. This is a new-business, near-full-suite opportunity (eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin Intake, ANA) that would ultimately need an MSA, Order Form, and a separately signed SOW, but there are no commercials, no legal, no InfoSec, and no procurement channel in motion. No Ariba contract-expiration date is on record; identifying the incumbent Ariba renewal/true-up date would be the single strongest forcing function to create a paper timeline. Visibility here depends entirely on first re-engaging the buyer.
sources: No commercials, legal, or InfoSec activity in the record

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well-articulated by the pain owner, Timothy Duprey (category manager), on 16 Sep 2025: 'poor end-to-end visibility across direct and indirect spend and contracts'; tail-spend leakage and 'high off contract purchases' despite Ariba P2P; supplier risk and compliance gaps ('environmental testing... sustainability'); Ariba integration and RFX under-used because progress 'slowed or stopped' with the S/4HANA migration; Jagger used for RFX and Sirion for CLM creating 'separate databases... with each of its own challenges and user experience'; and frustration with 'constant reinvention' from spinning off legacy SAP applications. The pain is real and current; the gap is that its owner cannot fund a fix.
sources: Avoma discovery call 16 Sep 2025, Timothy Duprey (multiple verbatim quotes)

champion.narrative: Timothy Duprey is the only engaged contact and reacted positively to Merlin Intake, Anna, and the CLM risk-scoring demo, asking substantive catalog/punch-out and contract-redline questions. But he disclaimed all authority ('I'm not driving anything'), has never introduced Zycus to the CET or any executive, and has not replied to three 2026 outreach emails (24 Mar, 20 May, 24 Jun). What developed: genuine product interest and personal AI curiosity. What is missing: authority, internal advocacy, and any recent response. His advocacy is now passive and cold; building him or a second empowered champion is the priority.
sources: Avoma discovery call 16 Sep 2025, Timothy Duprey: 'I'm advocating for more use of tools that leverage AI'
SF activities 24 Mar / 20 May / 24 Jun 2026 (rep emails, no buyer reply)

competition.narrative: The real contest is not a peer S2P vendor but the status quo. Chemours runs SAP Ariba (transactional P2P only, under-used because the S/4HANA migration stalled it), Jagger/JAGGAER for RFX, and Sirion for CLM, and a prior evaluation ended in keeping Ariba. Timothy is dissatisfied with the fragmentation and Sirion's dashboards, which is the wedge, but no formal project or bake-off exists, so inertia is the dominant threat. When an RFP eventually runs, expect SAP's account team to defend Ariba and possibly Coupa/Ivalua/GEP as peers. Zycus wins by consolidating the three incumbents onto one AI platform (PwC and Dow proof points) and by showing value without waiting on S/4HANA.
sources: Avoma discovery call 16 Sep 2025, Timothy Duprey on Ariba, Jagger, Sirion and the prior 'kept it' evaluation

deal_movement.summary: No forward buyer movement since the 16 Sep 2025 discovery call. 2026 shows only rep-side nudges and administrative field edits: three unanswered Clari emails (24 Mar, 20 May, 24 Jun 2026) and a 30 Jun close-date slip and Division change by the owner.

items.change: Close date pushed from 2026-09-16 to 2027-01-08 by Edward Dlugosz (owner-driven slip, no buyer commitment)
Division moved Zycus -> New Markets by Edward Dlugosz
Rep sent Horizon US 2026 invitation to Tim (Clari email); no buyer reply
Rep 're-touch' email asking about the 2026 project; no buyer reply
Rep follow-up email asking about the 2026 project; no buyer reply
Only genuine two-way buyer exchange: exploration/demo call with Timothy Duprey

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest current threat is do-nothing / status quo: Chemours ran a procurement evaluation 'a couple years back' and 'just kept' Ariba, no formal project has been chartered in ~9.5 months, and funding is tied to a stalled S/4HANA migration. The incumbents are SAP Ariba (transactional P2P, under-used, medium threat via inertia), Jagger/JAGGAER (RFX, medium), and Sirion (CLM, low - buyer explicitly dissatisfied). No peer S2P challenger has been named because no bake-off exists. Zycus is narratively well-positioned to consolidate all three onto one AI platform (PwC/Dow proof) but has no power access and a cold relationship. Recency note: no new call evidence this run, so competitor threat levels are carried forward unchanged.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / status quo
competitors.quote: A formal project may be formed maybe 2026. I'm not the project leader, part of the CET governing the project priorities... a lot of it was because of us migrating to s 4 HANA... that stopped.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 16 Sep 2025, Timothy Duprey
competitors.how_we_win: Manufacture a compelling event: co-build a quantified tail-spend savings case ($4-8M on $80-100M) and a phased, S/4HANA-independent Merlin Intake + Anna quick-win so the CET can justify chartering the project instead of deferring again.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: any progress at that time slowed or stopped... now we're back and looking at the management portal of using Ariba mainly at a transactional level... everybody has problems with integration with Ariba.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 16 Sep 2025, Timothy Duprey
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the unified AI platform that ends Ariba's integration/under-utilization pain and delivers value without waiting for S/4HANA; use PwC replacing Ariba as the proof.

competitors.name: Jagger (JAGGAER)
competitors.quote: we use YAGGER for that, or RFIs and RFQs and data gathering... we haven't leveraged Ariba into the RFX space.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 16 Sep 2025, Timothy Duprey
competitors.how_we_win: Fold RFX into a single AI-driven sourcing suite; PwC replaced both Jagger and Ariba with Zycus (65k users, one platform).

competitors.name: Sirion
competitors.quote: for contract management we use something that came over from our sales organization... Sirion... separate databases... you don't get an effective dashboard.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 16 Sep 2025, Timothy Duprey
competitors.how_we_win: Sirion is the weakest incumbent; demo the AI clause risk-scoring and metadata extraction (which landed on 16 Sep) as a step-change in usability within one integrated platform.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is strong on paper and weak in practice: Zycus maps well to every pain Timothy raised, but there is no project, no power access, and a cold relationship, so the fit is unvalidated.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a cold, single-threaded, early-stage deal whose only contact disclaims authority. The wins come from re-establishing contact, reaching real power, and manufacturing a reason to act now.
flags: Re-establish two-way contact before treating this as live pipeline: the only buyer conversation was 16 Sep 2025 and three 2026 nudges went unanswered.
Multi-thread past Timothy: he is a self-declared non-decision-maker, so map and reach the CET project owner and the CPO who set procurement priorities.
Manufacture a compelling event: bring a quantified tail-spend business case now so Zycus shapes any RFP before SAP's team defends Ariba.

items.name: Timothy Duprey

items.sentiment: Positive but cold - engaged and pain-aware on 16 Sep 2025, but no reply to three 2026 emails
items.risk: Only engaged contact and explicitly not a decision-maker ('not driving anything, part of the CET governing priorities'); has not introduced Zycus internally and has gone quiet. Advocacy is passive; needs re-activation and must be complemented by an empowered second champion.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 16 Sep 2025 + SF task/activity contact

champion_strength.summary: Timothy Duprey is a weak, now-cooling champion: genuinely interested in Zycus AI and articulate on pain, but he holds no authority ('not the project leader'), has never mobilized internal sponsorship, and has not responded to three 2026 outreach emails. He is the single point of contact. The priority is to re-activate him and, through him, reach the CET project owner and build a second champion closer to budget.
champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 16 Sep 2025; SF activities 24 Mar / 20 May / 24 Jun 2026 (unanswered)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The Zycus AI narrative (Merlin Intake on MS Teams, Anna for tail spend, AI clause risk-scoring) landed well with Timothy on 16 Sep 2025, but it has reached only one non-decision-maker and has not been carried to power, so positioning is strong in message and weak in account penetration.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 8.0 / Strong is well-founded: Timothy directly asked how AI can help his role and framed it as a way to 'catch up and be more efficient'. But the interest is personal and forward-dated ('maybe 2026'), not a funded AI initiative, so it is curiosity rather than active hunger.

critical_signals.text: No one beyond Timothy Duprey, a self-described non-decision-making stakeholder, has ever engaged, and the CET that charters projects is completely unmapped.
The only real conversation was the 16 Sep 2025 discovery demo, which landed well on Merlin Intake and Anna but produced no next step and no follow-up.
Chemours ran a procurement evaluation a couple of years ago and chose to keep Ariba, so the status quo, not a rival vendor, is what Zycus must beat.
No budget owner and no validated ROI: a $4-8M tail-spend savings case was floated but never confirmed, and funding hinges on the stalled S/4HANA migration.

scope_change.detail: No scope change; the only recent edits are a close-date slip (16 Sep 2026 to 8 Jan 2027) and a Division reassignment on 30 Jun 2026.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A single Sep-2025 discovery call with a self-described non-decision-maker that has since gone cold: three 2026 rep emails unanswered, no project chartered, and the close date quietly pushed to Jan 2027.

win_position.text: Zycus is early and uncontested but has no foothold in power: the only buyer ever engaged, Timothy Duprey, said on 16 Sep 2025 he is 'not driving anything, only a stakeholder', and no economic buyer or project leader is in the room.
The relationship has gone cold: the last genuine two-way exchange was the 16 Sep 2025 call and three 2026 follow-up emails (24 Mar, 20 May, 24 Jun) drew no reply.
No formal project exists; Timothy could only estimate 'maybe 2026' and the close date was pushed from 16 Sep 2026 to 8 Jan 2027 on 30 Jun by the owner, not by any buyer commitment.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is negative: one discovery call in 9.5 months, no second meeting booked, and the only recent activity is unanswered outbound email.
The buyer named no concrete dates beyond a vague 'maybe 2026' and never requested a follow-up meeting (16 Sep 2025).

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is minimal: Timothy engaged out of personal curiosity about AI, explicitly excluded himself from the CET that sets project priorities, and made no next-step commitment (16 Sep 2025).
He did react positively to Merlin Intake and Anna and asked substantive catalog, punch-out, and CLM-redline questions, showing genuine product interest (16 Sep 2025).

deal_risk.text: The dominant risk is do-nothing inertia: Chemours has run Ariba, Jagger, and Sirion for years and a prior evaluation 'a couple years back' ended with them keeping Ariba.
Budget and timing are hostage to the S/4HANA migration, which previously halted procurement progress and has no restart date (16 Sep 2025).

deal_momentum.evidence: One call in 9.5 months, no second meeting, three unanswered 2026 rep emails.

customer_commitment.evidence: Non-decision-maker contact, no next-step commitment, cold since Sep 2025.

deal_risk.evidence: Do-nothing bias, no EB, budget tied to stalled S/4HANA migration.

customer_preference.evidence: Timothy was curious about Zycus AI but only 'learning', excluded himself from the decision, asked for no Zycus-specific next step, and has not answered three 2026 follow-ups.

business_case.evidence: $80-100M tail spend stated by Timothy on 16 Sep 2025; rep positioned $4-8M Anna savings but the buyer never validated or owned any ROI figure.
